description: Geophysics and seismology - seismic wave propagation, reflection, Earth structure.

facts.context: For a Poisson solid with Poisson ratio nu=0.25, the P-wave to S-wave velocity ratio Vp/Vs equals:
distractors: sqrt(2) which is approximately 1.414
2.0 exactly for all elastic solids
sqrt(5) which is approximately 2.236

facts.context: The Rayleigh wave speed in a Poisson solid (nu=0.25) relative to the shear wave speed Vs is approximately:
facts.truth: 0.92 times Vs for a Poisson solid
distractors: 0.70 times Vs for a Poisson solid
1.05 times Vs for a Poisson solid
0.50 times Vs for a Poisson solid

facts.context: Love waves propagate in a layered medium only when:
facts.truth: a low-velocity surface layer overlies a higher-velocity half-space
distractors: a high-velocity surface layer overlies a lower-velocity half-space
both layers have identical shear wave velocities throughout
the surface layer is a liquid overlying a solid half-space

facts.context: The reflection coefficient R at normal incidence on a planar interface between two media with acoustic impedances Z1 and Z2 is:
facts.truth: R = (Z2 - Z1) / (Z2 + Z1) where Z = density times velocity
distractors: R = (Z2 + Z1) / (Z2 - Z1) where Z = density times velocity
R = (Z1 * Z2) / (Z1 + Z2) where Z = density times velocity
R = (Z2 - Z1) / (Z2 * Z1) where Z = density times velocity

facts.context: In the PREM reference Earth model, the P-wave velocity just below the Mohorovicic discontinuity in the upper mantle is approximately:
facts.truth: 8.1 km/s at the base of the crust-mantle boundary
distractors: 6.2 km/s at the base of the crust-mantle boundary
11.0 km/s at the base of the crust-mantle boundary
4.5 km/s at the base of the crust-mantle boundary

facts.context: Snell's law for seismic wave refraction across a velocity interface states:
facts.truth: sin(i)/V1 = sin(r)/V2 where i and r are incidence and refraction angles
distractors: sin(i)*V1 = sin(r)*V2 where i and r are incidence and refraction angles
cos(i)/V1 = cos(r)/V2 where i and r are incidence and refraction angles
tan(i)/V1 = tan(r)/V2 where i and r are incidence and refraction angles

facts.context: Total internal reflection of seismic waves occurs at the critical angle ic given by:
facts.truth: sin(ic) = V1/V2 when V1 is less than V2 across the interface
distractors: sin(ic) = V2/V1 when V1 is less than V2 across the interface
cos(ic) = V1/V2 when V1 is less than V2 across the interface
sin(ic) = V1/V2 when V1 is greater than V2 across the interface

facts.context: Shear waves (S-waves) encountering Earth's outer core are:
facts.truth: completely blocked because liquids have zero shear modulus
distractors: transmitted with reduced amplitude through the liquid core
converted entirely into compressional P-waves at the boundary
reflected and amplified by the liquid-solid impedance contrast

facts.context: Geometric spreading causes seismic wave amplitude to decay with distance r as:
facts.truth: 1/sqrt(r) for surface waves and 1/r for body waves in 3D
distractors: 1/r for surface waves and 1/r^2 for body waves in 3D media
1/r^2 for surface waves and 1/r^3 for body waves in 3D
1/sqrt(r) for body waves and 1/r for surface waves in 3D

facts.context: In a dispersive seismic medium, the group velocity and phase velocity are related by:
facts.truth: group velocity equals d(omega)/dk and differs from phase velocity omega/k
distractors: group velocity always equals phase velocity in all seismic media
group velocity equals omega/k while phase velocity is d(omega)/dk
group velocity equals twice the phase velocity in dispersive media

facts.context: The Gutenberg-Richter energy-magnitude relation for earthquakes states:
facts.truth: log10(E) = 1.5*M + 4.8 where E is energy in joules and M is magnitude
distractors: log10(E) = 1.0*M + 4.8 where E is energy in joules and M is magnitude
log10(E) = 2.0*M + 4.8 where E is energy in joules and M is magnitude
log10(E) = 1.5*M + 11.8 where E is energy in ergs and M is magnitude

facts.context: Seismic wave attenuation described by the quality factor Q causes amplitude to decay as:
facts.truth: exp(-pi*f*t/Q) where f is frequency and t is travel time
distractors: exp(-pi*Q*t/f) where f is frequency and t is travel time
exp(-2*pi*f/Q) where f is frequency independent of travel time
exp(-f*t/2*pi*Q) where f is frequency and t is travel time
